title: UX Report
description: Frontend-agnostic, versioned data model for UX audit reports. One document = one report for one target at one point in time. Designed so any renderer (HTML, React, Angular, PDF) consumes the same document without transformation.

schemaVersion.description: Semver of THIS schema. Renderers branch on the major version. Additive changes bump minor; breaking changes bump major. Never reuse a version number.

reportId.description: Stable unique id for this report instance (UUID/ULID). Used by report-history and as a PDF/export filename seed.

reportType.description: Discriminator for future report families. A renderer can show type-specific sections. 'ux_audit' is the general heuristic+a11y review; the others are reserved so adding them later is non-breaking.

status.description: Lifecycle state. Lets the platform list drafts vs. delivered reports without a separate store.

locale.description: BCP-47 language tag of the PRIMARY rendered prose in this document (problem/recommendation text etc.).

i18n.description: Optional translation overlay. Maps a locale to an object of dotted JSON-Pointers→translated string, so a single report can be shown in multiple languages without duplicating the whole document. v1 may leave this empty; the field exists so adding localization later is non-breaking.

client.description: Who the report is FOR. Separated from 'target' so one client can own many targets/reports (multi-client platform).

clientId.description: Stable id; groups reports under one client in history.

branding.description: Per-report branding overlay (white-label). Renderer falls back to platform defaults when a field is absent. Kept as semantic tokens, NOT raw CSS, so any frontend/PDF engine can map them.

primaryColor.description: Hex. Maps to the renderer's primary token.

fontFamily.description: Font stack name; renderer resolves loading.

theme.description: Preferred visual theme hint for renderers. 'auto' respects user/system preference.

target.description: What was audited. A 'target' is one website/app/screen-set. Multiple targets per client; multiple reports per target over time.

meta.description: Provenance and method. Critical for trust/defensibility and for the 'report history' feature (compare runs).

version.description: Report content revision (independent of schemaVersion). Increment when findings change.

previousReportId.description: Links to the prior report for the same target → enables history/diff/trend.

methodology.description: How findings were produced — sets the confidence ceiling honestly.

limitations.description: What could NOT be verified with the sources above (e.g. runtime performance). Renderer shows this prominently to keep the report defensible.

summary.description: Top-of-report narrative. 'aiGenerated' flag + 'aiModel' make AI-summaries auditable and let the UI badge them. Human-edited summaries set aiGenerated=false.

scoring.description: Quantitative layer. Categories are DATA, not hardcoded, so report types can define their own dimensions. Overall is derived from weighted category scores; the weights ship with the report so the number is reproducible and explainable.

bands.description: Named ranges → drive badge color/label without renderer hardcoding thresholds.

overall.description: Weighted overall score. SHOULD equal round(sum(category.score * category.weight)/sum(weights)). Stored explicitly so renderers/PDF don't recompute.

overallBand.description: Resolved band label for 'overall' (denormalized for convenience).

key.description: Stable machine key (snake_case), e.g. 'accessibility'. Used for i18n + diffing across reports.

weight.description: Relative weight for the overall calc. Renderer can also show as importance.

band.description: Resolved band label for this category.

rationale.description: Why this score — keeps numbers defensible.

previousScore.description: Same category in previousReportId → trend arrows.

strengths.description: Explicitly positive findings. Same shape spirit as findings but lighter — a premium report leads with what works.

categoryKey.description: Optional link to a scoring category.

quickWins.description: Curated high-value/low-effort actions. Each MAY reference a finding by id (single source of truth) OR stand alone.

findingId.description: If set, this quick win is the actionable face of that finding.

recommendationsRoadmap.description: Long-term / strategic improvements grouped by horizon. Separate from quickWins so the UI can render a timeline.

findings.description: The core. Each finding is self-contained and renderer-ready. Order is NOT significance — use severity + 'rank' for ordering.

attachments.description: Top-level media not bound to a single finding (e.g. cover screenshot, methodology PDF). Per-finding media live on the finding.

tags.description: Free-form labels for filtering across the platform.

confidence.description: How sure the issue exists given the methodology sources. high=verified in code/test; medium=strongly inferable; low=needs runtime/user data to confirm.

evidence.description: Concrete proof. Keeps findings defensible and lets the UI deep-link to the offending code/region.

locations.description: Where in the artifact the issue lives.

measurements.description: Quantified facts (e.g. contrast ratios). Structured so charts can read them.

media.description: An attachment/screenshot. URL-based so storage backend is swappable; 'placeholder' lets a report be authored before images exist.

placeholder.description: true = image not yet captured; renderer shows a labelled slot.

alt.description: Accessibility text for the media itself.

annotations.description: Optional callouts on the image (x/y in %).

id.description: Stable id (e.g. 'F-P1'). Referenced by quickWins/roadmap and across report history for diffing.

rank.description: Optional explicit display order within its severity group.

status.description: Lets the same document track remediation over time (history feature).

recommendation.description: How to fix — specific and actionable.

uxPrinciple.description: Related, named UX principle (e.g. 'Nielsen #3: User control & freedom').

isOpportunity.description: true = proactive enhancement (delight/clarity/trust), not a defect.
